title: Semantic HTML carries built-in behavior
description: Native elements like button, a, and input bring keyboard handling, focusability, and roles that a div with ARIA must reimplement by hand.
body: Use when deciding between a native element and a styled div/span. A native <button> is focusable, fires on Enter/Space, and exposes a button role for free; a <div role=button> reimplements none of that and needs tabindex, key handlers, and an explicit role. Do not rebuild native semantics on generic elements unless truly necessary. Actions: reach for the native element first and only add ARIA when no native element fits. Validation signals include keyboard operation working without custom handlers and a correct role in the accessibility tree. Risk boundary: a fake button missing keyboard support is unusable without a mouse; verify keyboard operability before shipping any custom control.
